title: 10 best herbal essential oils for ulcerative colitis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for ulcerative colitis are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate symptoms of the inflammatory bowel disease. These oils, such as peppermint, chamomile, and frankincense, possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm inflammation and reduce pain. By inhaling or applying these oils topically, individuals with ulcerative colitis can experience relief from symptoms, improved digestion, and enhanced quality of life, allowing them to enjoy everyday activities with greater ease and comfort.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint essential oil reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, which helps to alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and cramping associated with Ulcerative Colitis.
Antispasmodic properties: The oil's ability to relax smooth muscle spasms can help to reduce bowel movements frequency, urgency, and pain caused by Ulcerative Colitis.

Antioxidant properties: Peppermint essential oil contains antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and oxidative stress associated with Ulcerative Colitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. The oil's menthol content relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for better digestion and reducing symptoms such as abdominal pain and cramping. Additionally, peppermint oil's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial overgrowth in the gut, which is often associated with ulcerative colitis. This makes peppermint oil a potential natural remedy for managing symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation in the gut and alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Caryophyllene oxide: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, protecting the gut from oxidative damage and promoting healing of the colon.
Menthol: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in menthone and menthol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Stems: Contain a high amount of menthol and menthone, contributing to the overall therapeutic benefits of peppermint essential oil for ulcerative colitis.
Roots: Rich in antioxidants and volatile oils, which can help reduce inflammation and promote digestive health in individuals with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 pounds of fresh leaves and stems of mentha x piperita at peak oil production in morning.
Clean the fresh plant material by gently washing it with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the plant material using a distillation apparatus with a water to plant ratio of 3:1 for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled oil from the distillation apparatus and separate it from water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a layer of activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve its clarity.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal ginger essential oil's anti-inflammatory compound, gingerol, reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain and diarrhea associated with ulcerative colitis.
Antioxidant property: Ginger's antioxidant properties protect the colon from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of ulcerative colitis.
Digestive stimulant property: The essential oil stimulates digestion and relieves symptoms such as bloating, gas, and constipation often experienced by individuals with ulcerative colitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. Ginger oil contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut. It also has antioxidant properties, which help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by inflammation. Additionally, ginger oil has been found to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of nausea and vomiting, making it a potential natural remedy for managing ulcerative colitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Zingiberene: A sesquiterpene, Zingiberene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the colon and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
6-Gingerol: A phenolic compound, 6-Gingerol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help protect the colon from oxidative stress and inflammation associated with ulcerative colitis.

Curcumenol: A sesquiterpene, Curcumenol has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the colon, alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make essential oils for ulcerative colitis as they contain active compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.

Barks: The barks of Zingiber officinale are occasionally used to make essential oils for ulcerative colitis, but it is less common due to its lower concentration of active compounds compared to the rhizomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes from mature plants in late summer or early autumn.
Distill the harvested rhizomes using steam distillation apparatus at a temperature of 100c for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a dark glass container and filter it through a paper filter.
Add a small amount of distilled water to the filtered essential oil to achieve a desired consistency and quality.
Store the final herbal zingiber officinale essential oil in a sealed glass container in a cool dark place.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcuminoids, can reduce inflammation in the colon and alleviate symptoms like diarrhea, abdominal pain, and cramping.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of turmeric essential oil help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to Ulcerative Colitis development and progression.

Gut-soothing: Turmeric essential oil's active compounds, such as curcumin and volatile oils, have been shown to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the gut, reducing symptoms like rectal bleeding, itching, and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil contains curcumin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the colon and promote healing. Research has demonstrated that curcumin can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to the symptoms of ulcerative colitis. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Curcuma longa essential oils may help alleviate symptoms and improve quality of life for individuals with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A phenolic compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, contributing to the management of ulcerative colitis symptoms.

Turmerone: A sesquiterpene compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, promoting healing and reducing symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes from Curcuma longa are used to make essential oils because they contain the highest concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are used to make essential oils due to their rich content of essential oils and volatile compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Leaves: Leaves of Curcuma longa are used to make essential oils because they contain bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help manage ulcerative colitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of curcuma longa rhizomes and 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil.
Steam distill the curcuma longa rhizomes at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the carrier oil using a separating funnel or centrifuge.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve its quality.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

Anxiolytic: English Lavender essential oil's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for Ulcerative Colitis flares and can also make symptoms worse.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's sesquiterpenes and linalool content have been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut, promoting healing and alleviating symptoms. Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, and alleviate symptoms associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain the most potent essential oil, which helps alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia contain essential oils that help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.
Stems: The stems of the plant may contain some essential oils, but their primary use is for the essential oil that is extracted from the flowers and leaves for ulcerative colitis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure maximum oil content.
Steam distill 100 grams of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in 3 kilograms of water for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the condensed water through decantation and filter the oil through a coffee filter.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place.
Check the oil for quality and purity by performing a gas chromatography and mass spectrometry test after 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the ripe fruit of the citrus x aurantium plant in the morning, after the dew has evaporated, to minimize moisture content.
Peel the harvested fruit in a food processor, using 10 kg of peels per 20 liters of solvent, such as hexane or ethanol, to obtain the essential oil.
Steam distill the peels in a stainless steel distillation apparatus, using a ratio of 1 part peels to 3 parts water, at 60-80°c for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the water and solvent mixture, using a hydrotrope, such as glycerin, to facilitate separation.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 0.2 micrometer filter to remove any remaining impurities and obtain the final product.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves from organic farms or your own garden in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or by spreading them out in a single layer and allowing them to air dry for 7-10 days.
Steam-distill the dried melissa officinalis leaves with 10% water and 90% distilled steam at a temperature of 100°c to 105°c for 30-40 minutes to extract the essential oil.

Filter the distillation mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a glass vial and discard the solids, then store the filtered essential oil in an amber glass bottle away from direct sunlight.

Use the melissa officinalis essential oil topically by mixing 5-10 drops with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and applying it to the affected area up to three times a day for at least two weeks.

Dry the leaves in a warm place for at least 24 hours or until crisp and brittle.

Steep the mixture in a dark glass jar for four to six weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard solids, then store the essential oil.

Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for distillation.
Steam-distill the chopped eucalyptus globulus mixture using 100 ml of water and a glass distillation apparatus for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separating funnel and collect 10 ml of eucalyptus globulus essential oil.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid at room temperature.

Dry the leaves using shade or machine drying at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a still at 100°c for 4 hours with 200g of water.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles at 5°c.
Use the essential oil within 6 months, adding 5 drops to tea or food twice daily.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of pelargonium graveolens at dawn or just before sunset for maximum oil content and aroma retention.
Clean the harvested leaves thoroughly by rinsing them with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the cleaned pelargonium graveolens leaves in a 10:1 ratio with pure water at atmospheric pressure for 4 hours to extract the essential oils.
Filter the resulting oil mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to separate the essential oil from the hydrosol and plant material.
Store the extracted pelargonium graveolens essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature away from direct sunlight and heat sources for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum leaves, ensuring they are free of pesticides and other contaminants, within one year of growth.
Steam distill the harvested cinnamomum verum leaves using a 10% leaf-to-water ratio and a temperature range of 100-150°f for four hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a paper coffee filter to remove any impurities, ensuring the final product has a minimum of 2% cineol content.
Mix the filtered essential oil with 10ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil to create a blend suitable for topical application, taking approximately 30 minutes to prepare.
Store the finished cinnamomum verum essential oil blend in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature, allowing it to mature for at least six months before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia dracunculus leaves at dawn or before full sun for optimal oil quality and potency.

Chop 500 grams of washed and dried artemisia dracunculus leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for steam distillation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried syzygium aromaticum bark and grind it into a fine powder weighing approximately 100 grams.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ulcerative colitis that are treated with herbal essential oils are irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and Crohn's disease. Certain essential oils, such as peppermint, fennel, ginger, and turmeric, have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help alleviate symptoms like abdominal pain, diarrhea, constipation, and bloating. These oils may also help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal essential oils that help with ulcerative colitis is a blend of anti-inflammatory and soothing oils. Aromatherapists recommend combining peppermint oil, known for its calming effects on the digestive system, with ginger oil, which reduces inflammation and promotes digestion. Additionally, frankincense oil can be added for its anti-inflammatory properties, while lavender oil helps to reduce stress and promote relaxation. This blend can be applied topically or inhaled to help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
